tutorial.title: Help for this app
tutorial.home_info_paragraph: Home is the main entry point. From here you choose whether to register, log in, or return to the main application flow.
tutorial.home_feature_1: Quick access to Register and Login.
tutorial.home_feature_3: Access to Settings to change visual and behavior preferences.
tutorial.home_feature_4: Link to the Help and tutorial section.
tutorial.home_important_paragraph: The settings button lets you adjust visual elements and general options from any screen where it appears.
tutorial.home_language_description: Screenshot of the language selector and available options.
tutorial.home_settings_description: Screenshot of the settings modal where preferences can be changed.
tutorial.home_help_description: Screenshot of the help/instructions section and quick links.
tutorial.register_feature_1: Create a new account by entering your details and choosing a password.
tutorial.register_feature_2: Field validation on both the client and the server.
tutorial.register_feature_3: Clear error messages for invalid data.
tutorial.register_settings_description: Screenshot of the settings panel from Register.
tutorial.register_help_description: Screenshot of contextual help from Register.
tutorial.login_feature_1: Access for registered users using username and password.
tutorial.login_feature_2: Error handling for incorrect data or server issues.
tutorial.login_feature_3: Hints to recover or correct access data.
tutorial.login_settings_description: Screenshot of the settings panel from Login.
tutorial.login_help_description: Screenshot of contextual help from Login.
tutorial.game_info_paragraph: The Game window brings together everything you need during a match: the triangular board, accumulated points, active size and difficulty, the turn timer, the information panel, the quick bot guide, and the shortcuts for history, profile, friends, settings, help, restart, surrender and exit. Everything is arranged so you can play and check details without losing track of the match.
tutorial.game_feature_1: Top bar with quick access to profile, points, size, difficulty, surrender, restart, history, help, settings, friends and exit.
tutorial.game_feature_2: Match size selector to prepare a smaller or larger game before starting.
tutorial.game_feature_3: Difficulty selector to change the bot behavior and the pace of the match.
tutorial.game_feature_4: Centered points badge showing the XP accumulated during the whole session.
tutorial.game_feature_5: Turn timer that appears while the match is active and shows the time left for each move.
tutorial.game_feature_6: Interactive triangular board: only real, empty cells can be clicked, and each click sends the correct linear index to the backend.
tutorial.game_feature_7: Floating information panel with the key match data: difficulty, size, victory points and current rival.
tutorial.game_feature_8: Quick bot guide with the objective of the match and the basic instructions to follow while playing.
tutorial.game_important_paragraph:
- Size and difficulty selectors are locked once the match has started.
- The surrender button is only available when a match is in progress and there is no winner yet.
- The timer disappears when it is not active or when the match ends.
- The board uses triangular row numbering, so the index sent to the backend does not match the exact visual position of the cell.
tutorial.s4_1_desc: The top bar gathers the main actions for the match: profile, accumulated points, board size, difficulty, history, friends, help, settings, surrender, restart and exit. It keeps the most useful controls visible so you can manage the game without leaving the screen.
tutorial.game_capture_1: The top bar screenshot shows the shortcuts for profile, points, size, difficulty, history, friends, help, settings, surrender, restart and exit.
tutorial.s4_2_desc: The match size selector lets you choose the board size before the game begins. A smaller board usually means a shorter match, while a larger one gives you more room to play.
tutorial.game_capture_2: The size selector screenshot shows the options you can use before starting the match.
tutorial.s4_3_desc: The difficulty selector changes how the bot behaves and how demanding the match will be. You can use it to pick an easier game or a more challenging one depending on your experience.
tutorial.game_capture_3: The difficulty screenshot shows the available levels and how the bot difficulty is chosen.
tutorial.s4_4_desc: The centered points badge shows the total points you have accumulated during the session. It helps you keep track of your progress and see how far you have advanced.
tutorial.game_capture_4: The points screenshot shows the center badge with the total XP earned by the player.
tutorial.s4_5_desc: The turn timer tells you how much time you have left to make your move. If it reaches zero, the turn is resolved automatically, so it is worth watching during the match.
tutorial.game_capture_5: The timer screenshot shows the countdown bar and the remaining time for the current turn.
tutorial.s4_6_desc: The triangular board only lets you click real, empty cells. Each click sends the correct board index to the backend so the move is placed in the proper position.
tutorial.game_capture_6: The board screenshot shows a selectable cell and the visual state of the triangular grid.
tutorial.s4_7_desc: The floating information panel summarizes the match at a glance: difficulty, board size, points needed to win and the current rival. It gives you context without needing another screen.
tutorial.game_capture_7: The information panel screenshot shows the difficulty, size, victory points and rival of the match.
tutorial.s4_8_desc: The quick bot guide and the profile, friends and exit shortcuts are grouped on the right side so you can check help, open your profile, interact with friends or leave the match quickly.
tutorial.game_capture_8: The right side screenshot shows the quick guide, profile access, friends, history and exit button.
tutorial.s4_9_desc: The match history lets you review previous games and check details such as the rival, board size, difficulty, date and final result. It is useful for seeing how you played over time.
tutorial.game_capture_9: The history screenshot shows the filters and the list of saved matches.
tutorial.s4_10_desc: When the match ends, this window appears with the final summary and a confirmation button. It tells you that the game is over and lets you return to the menu or start another match.
tutorial.game_capture_10: The end-of-match screenshot shows the modal that appears when a game finishes.
tutorial.s4_11_desc: If you win, the victory window shows the positive result and confirms the end of the match. From there you can close the notice and keep the result as part of your progress.
tutorial.game_capture_11: The victory screenshot shows the positive result window after winning.
tutorial.s4_12_desc: If you lose, the defeat window shows the final result and lets you close the message before trying again. It is only the closing summary of the match, so you can start over right away.
tutorial.game_capture_12: The defeat screenshot shows the final result window after losing.
tutorial.home_what_text: Home is the main entry point. From here you choose whether to register, log in, or return to the main application flow.
tutorial.home_can_text: If you are new, start with Register. If you already have an account, use Login. This separation avoids confusion and makes the journey clearer.
tutorial.home_structure_text: In Home you first see the title, the logo, and the main options. Language, settings, and help stay below so the screen reads naturally from top to bottom.
tutorial.home_settings_text: The settings button lets you adjust visual elements and general application options from any screen where it appears.
tutorial.home_language_text: This button opens the language selector so you can change the interface language at any time.
tutorial.settings_desc: The screenshot shows access to settings.
tutorial.register_fields_text: In Register you create your account. You must fill in username, nickname, date of birth, language and password with confirmation.
tutorial.register_errors_text: If you leave required fields empty, or if the password and confirmation do not match, the form will show a warning for you to fix before submitting.
tutorial.register_server_text: If this message appears, it is not something you need to fix. It usually indicates a temporary server problem or internal issue, and you should wait a moment and try again later.
tutorial.register_security_text: Do not include sensitive personal information in public fields. Use data needed to play and maintain your account, avoiding sharing credentials or private data.
tutorial.register_language_text: From this button you can change the registration screen language without leaving it.
tutorial.register_empty_desc: The form before you fill in your account details.
tutorial.register_empty_space_desc: The warning when required fields are left empty.
tutorial.register_error_pswd_desc: The message when the password is not valid.
tutorial.login_what: What data you need to enter
tutorial.login_what_text: In Login you must enter the username and password you registered with.
tutorial.login_validation_text: If any field is empty, the request will not be sent and you will see a validation warning.
tutorial.login_errors_text: If the username or password are incorrect, an authentication error will appear. If a connection error appears, it is usually temporary and does not depend on your data. Wait a moment and try again.
tutorial.login_language_text: From this button you can change the sign-in screen language without leaving it.
tutorial.login_tip_1: Check uppercase and lowercase in username and password.
tutorial.login_tip_2: Fix the marked field and try again.
tutorial.login_tip_3: If it persists, check your connection status.
tutorial.login_error_data_desc: The warning shown when the username or password do not match a registered account.
tutorial.login_error_server_desc: If you see this warning, wait a moment and try again.
tutorial.login_good_desc: Session started correctly and ready to enter the application.
tutorial.example_1_text: Example text to check index scrolling.
tutorial.example_2_text: Second test point with simple text.
tutorial.example_3_text: Third example point to extend the list.
tutorial.example_4_text: Fourth test point to force vertical scroll.
tutorial.s4_13_desc: This last game subsection shows the settings and help access points that you can use to adjust the app or reopen the guide while playing. It appears at the end so you can review the match itself first and then find the tools that help you change options or check instructions without leaving the context of the game.
tutorial.game_capture_9: This screenshot shows the match history list with filters and recorded games.
tutorial.game_capture_10: This screenshot shows the modal that appears when the match ends.
tutorial.game_capture_11: This screenshot shows the victory modal with the positive result.
tutorial.game_capture_12: This screenshot shows the defeat modal with the final result.
